subject: [RFC PATCH 02/45] mm: page_alloc: per-cpu pageblock buddy allocator

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch introduces a per-cpu pageblock buddy allocator to improve page allocation performance on large machines. It addresses two common patterns of contention: affinity violations and concurrent exits, by extending the per-cpu (PCP) allocator to operate on whole pageblocks with ownership tracking. The PCP now claims pageblocks from the zone buddy, splits them locally, and tags pages with their owning CPU, eliminating affinity violations and reducing lock contention. The patch also introduces a four-phase refill approach for the PCP, which includes recovering owned fragments, claiming whole pageblocks, grabbing sub-pageblock chunks, and traditional __rmqueue() with migratetype fallback. This allows for more efficient allocation and merging of pages, reducing fragmentation and improving overall performance.
